calculator.description: Perform mathematical calculations on a list of numbers. Use this for finding the sum, product, minimum, maximum, or average (mean) of a dataset. Useful for basic statistics and arithmetic.

get_weather.description: Retrieves the current weather forecast. Use this to check if it is raining, snowing, sunny, or to get the temperature in a specific city (e.g., Tokyo).

get_stock_data.description: Fetches real-time or historical stock market data. Use this to check share prices, stock performance, and financial metrics for a company by its symbol or name (e.g., AAPL, Apple).

read_recent_emails.description: Retrieves recent emails from the user's Gmail inbox. Use this to read the latest messages, check for unread emails, or search for specific emails from a sender or with a specific subject.

send_email.description: Sends a new email from the user's Gmail account. Use this to compose and send messages to recipients. Requires a recipient email address, subject, and body text. Always resolve the email address via 'list_contacts' first if only a name is given.

list_calendar_events.description: Retrieves the user's personal daily agenda, upcoming meetings, and appointments from Google Calendar. Use this to check availability, see what is planned for today or tomorrow, and manage time.

create_calendar_event.description: Schedules a new event, meeting, or appointment in Google Calendar. Use this to book time slots, set up reminders for specific dates and times, and invite attendees.

list_contacts.description: Searches the user's Google Contacts address book. Use this to find contact details like email addresses or phone numbers for a specific person (e.g., 'What is John's email?').

get_current_playback.description: Checks what song or audio is currently playing on Spotify. Use this to identify the active track, artist, and album.

play_spotify.description: Resumes media playback on the active Spotify device. Use this to unpause music or continue listening.

pause_spotify.description: Pauses the current media playback on Spotify. Use this to stop music or audio temporarily.

next_track_spotify.description: Skips to the next song or track in the Spotify queue. Use this to jump forward in the playlist.

previous_track_spotify.description: Returns to the previous song or track in the Spotify history. Use this to go back or replay a song.

start_spotify_playback.description: Starts playing specific music on Spotify. Use this to play a song, album, artist, or playlist by providing its URI. Can also initiate playback on a specific context.

list_spotify_devices.description: Lists all available Spotify Connect devices. Use this to see where music can be played (e.g., laptop, phone, smart speaker) and get device IDs for transfer.

set_spotify_device.description: Transfers Spotify playback to a different device. Use this to switch music from one device (e.g., phone) to another (e.g., speaker).

search_spotify.description: Searches the Spotify catalog for music. Use this to find tracks, albums, artists, or playlists by name and get their URIs for playback.

get_time.description: Retrieves the current local time for a specific location, city, or timezone. Use this to check 'What time is it in Tokyo?' or 'current time'.

brightness_control.description: Adjusts the screen brightness level. Use this to dim the screen, make it brighter, or set it to a specific percentage.

clipboard_manager.description: Manages the system clipboard. Use this to read the current copied text (paste) or set new text into the clipboard (copy).

close_window.description: Closes a specific application window. Use this to terminate or quit a window by its title.

create_note.description: Creates a new text file. Use this to jot down quick thoughts, save memos, or use as a scratchpad. Use this for text storage, NOT for time-based alerts.

delete_scheduled_task.description: Removes a scheduled task from Windows Task Scheduler. Use this to cancel future automated actions.

find_files.description: Searches the hard drive for specific files. Use this to find PDFs, invoices, documents, images, or executables by name.

focus_window.description: Brings an open window to the foreground. Use this to bring apps like 'Firefox', 'Chrome', or 'Spotify' to the front/focus.

get_system_info.description: Retrieves detailed hardware and software specifications. Use this to get information about the OS, CPU, memory (RAM), and disk space.

list_open_windows.description: Lists all currently open application windows. Use this to see what programs are running and visible on the desktop.

list_scheduled_tasks.description: Lists technical background jobs and automated scripts in the Windows Task Scheduler. Use this to manage system automation and cron-like tasks, NOT for personal meetings.

maximize_window.description: Maximizes a window to fill the screen. Use this to expand an application window.

minimize_window.description: Minimizes a window to the taskbar. Use this to hide an application window without closing it.

open_app.description: Launches a desktop application executable. Use this to start or run programs like 'Firefox', 'Spotify', 'Calculator', 'Notepad', or 'Chrome'. Use this to open the app window, not to control content inside it.

open_webpage.description: Navigates to a specific URL or website address (e.g., google.com, youtube.com). Use this to visit a page directly, NOT to search for information.

play_song.description: Searches for and plays a song on YouTube in the browser. Use this to listen to specific music or videos on the web.

rate_limit.description: Internal tool for rate limiting operations. Limits how often a function can be called.

schedule_task.description: Schedules a command or script to run at a specific time. Use this to automate tasks using Windows Task Scheduler.

screenshot.description: Takes a screenshot of the entire screen. Use this to capture the current display state and save it as an image file.

search_web.description: Queries a search engine (Google/Bing) to find answers, pasta recipes, or information. Use this when looking for facts or unknown websites.

secure_operation.description: Internal tool for validating inputs and logging operations for security.

set_reminder.description: Sets a time-based alert or notification. Use this to get reminded at a specific time or after a duration. NOT for writing notes.

show_notification.description: Displays a desktop notification. Use this to show a pop-up message to the user.

volume_control.description: Adjusts the system audio volume. Use this to mute, unmute, increase, decrease, or set the volume to a specific level.

system_power.description: Controls system power states. Use this to shutdown, reboot, restart the computer, sleep, hibernate, or lock the PC.
